highlights.title: Grok Build (xAI) support
highlights.description: TokenTelemetry now tracks xAI's Grok Build coding agent — sessions, context usage, tool calls, permission decisions, and plan mode — with a dedicated forensics card. That's 11 agents tracked in total.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot tracking restored
highlights.description: Recent VS Code versions changed how Copilot chats are stored on disk, which made new sessions read as zero. TokenTelemetry now reads the new format, so your recent Copilot sessions show up again.

highlights.title: Smarter 'Back' from a session
highlights.description: Opening a session and hitting Back now returns you to the page you came from — e.g. the project you were viewing — instead of always jumping to the dashboard.

highlights.title: Per-project & per-agent budgets
highlights.description: Set a monthly, weekly, or rolling-30-day spend limit on a project — overall and per coding agent (e.g. Claude $60, Codex unlimited). Track usage in dollars or tokens.

highlights.title: Budget at a glance
highlights.description: Open any project to see spend vs limit: a budget pill in the header and a progress card under Insights, with a per-agent breakdown.

highlights.title: Notification center
highlights.description: A bell in the sidebar collects budget alerts with an unread count; new alerts surface once at the top, then wait in the bell. Mark read or clear all when you're done.

highlights.title: Summarizer settings
highlights.description: Pick a backend (Ollama / Codex / Claude / Gemini / Qwen / Antigravity) and choose a specific model for it. Avoids 'incorrect API key' errors when your account doesn't have GPT-5, or 'timed out' when Ollama's default model is too big for your hardware.

highlights.title: Configurable ports
highlights.description: start.sh now accepts --port (frontend) and --api-port (backend), e.g. start.sh --port 4000 --api-port 9000. The frontend is told the backend's location automatically via NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE, so fetches always reach the right place.

highlights.title: Hermes Agent dashboard
highlights.description: New sub-pages for Gateway, Memory, Profiles, Schedules, Skills, Soul, and Tools — all read directly from your local ~/.hermes state.
